{"society_power": 9.0, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Devolution and rights culture vs. surveillance/security state expansion", "reasoning": "Society was strengthened by devolution, the Human Rights Act, FOI, and mass mobilization, but simultaneously constrained by an expanding surveillance/security state, leaving its net position roughly stable; the state gained coercive and economic capacity (anti-terror powers, 2008 bailouts) while losing legitimacy and central authority to devolution, netting near-zero change."}
